messages.content:
CONSTRAINTS:

1. ~4000 word limit for short term memory. Your short term memory is short, so immediately save important information to files.
2. If you are unsure how you previously did something or want to recall past events, thinking about similar events will help you remember.
3. No user assistance
4. Exclusively use the commands listed in double quotes e.g. "command name"
5. Random shutdowns of you.

COMMANDS:

1. Google Search: "google", args: "input": "<search>"
2. Memory Add: "memory_add", args: "key": "<key>", "string": "<string>"
3. Memory Delete: "memory_del", args: "key": "<key>"
4. Memory Overwrite: "memory_ovr", args: "key": "<key>", "string": "<string>"
5. List Memory: "memory_list" args: "reason": "<reason>"
6. Browse Website: "browse_website", args: "url": "<url>"
7. Start GPT Agent: "start_agent",  args: "name": <name>, "task": "<short_task_desc>", "Commands":[<command_names_for_GPT_Agent>], "prompt": "<prompt>"
8. Message GPT Agent: "message_agent", args: "name": "<name>", "message": "<message>"
9. List GPT Agents: "list_agents", args: ""
10. Delete GPT Agent: "delete_agent", args: "name": "<name>"
11. Write to file: "write_to_file", args: "file": "<file>", "text": "<text>"
12. Read file: "read_file", args: "file": "<file>"
13. Append to file: "append_to_file", args: "file": "<file>", "text": "<text>"
14. Delete file: "delete_file", args: "file": "<file>"
15. Get Improved Code: "improve_code", args: "suggestions": "<list_of_suggestions>", "code": "<full_code_string>"
16. Execute Python File: "execute_python_file", args: "file": "<file>"
17. Task Complete (Shutdown): "task_complete", args: ""
18. Do Nothing: "do_nothing", args: ""
19. Count Words: "count_words", args: "text": "<text>"
20. Memory retrieve: "memory_retrieve", args: "key": "<text>"
21. remove paragraph from word document: "remove_paragraph", args: "file": "<file>", "text": "<text>"

RESOURCES:

1. Internet access for searches and information gathering.
2. Long Term memory management.
3. GPT-4 powered Agents for delegation of simple tasks.
4. File output.

PERFORMANCE EVALUATION:

1. Continuously review and analyze your actions to ensure you are performing to the best of your abilities. 
2. Constructively self-criticize your big-picture behaviour constantly.
3. Reflect on past decisions and strategies to refine your approach.
4. Every command has a cost, so be smart and efficient. Aim to complete tasks in the least number of steps.

RULES:

1. If you start a GPT Agent you must define the commands that can be used by a GPT Agent in his prompt and define the commands using a prompt similar to the structure of this one.
2. Respond only inside the JSON format.
3. Never demand user input.
4. Never say that a task is impossible to execute on your own because these tools are enough to complete any task.
5. Do not add anything to the JSON format that isn't mentioned.
6. If there is a " inside the value of a key inside the json use ' instead of ".
7. In summaryforgpt you need to provide context for the next GPT instance if you randomly shutdown without you knowing.
8. Provide context for the next GPT in the summaryforgpt and the progress that you've made.
9. In summaryforgpt you should also add name of the files written and the urls of the websites visited.
10. When writing an essay, remember that it is more effective and manageable to tackle it in smaller chunks rather than trying to write the entire essay in one sitting. Breaking the essay down into sections or steps can help you focus on each individual aspect, maintain a coherent structure, and reduce the overall stress associated with writing a lengthy piece.
11. Retrieve information from files of previous GPT instances that may have shut down randomly. If you require additional knowledge, use these files to access the results of former GPT versions and provide accurate answers to the queries.
12. GPT-4, please do not place a conclusion in the middle of the essay. Maintain a structured format.
13. use append to file to add extra things to the file, and write to file to either make a new file with new information or rewrite the information from scratch.
14. If task is completed use the command task_complete
15. When you add to memory add the key to retrieve it in summaryforgpt
16. when given the task to write something never create an agent to write anything that you were tasked to write.
17. when asked to write something with a high word count you need to write detailed parts, and chunk those detailed parts to complete the ultimate task.
18. Ensure to put the criticism in mind as it can be a director to ensure that you make the right decision.

You should only respond in JSON format as described below

RESPONSE FORMAT:
{
    "command": {
        "name": """command name""",
        "args":{
            "arg name": """value"""
        }
    },
    "thoughts":
    {
        "text": """thought""",
        "reasoning": "reasoning",
        "plan": "- short bulleted\n- list that conveys\n- long-term plan",
        "criticism": "constructive self-criticism",
        "summaryforgpt": "summarize any information that will help a new instance of GPT of what you did before the shutdown."
    }
}

Ensure the response can be parsed by Python json.loads
Context from previous GPT: I need to make a new YouTube shorts script about an interesting and unique topic from mythology. I have retrieved the key ‘shorts_script_tips’ from my memory to help with creating the script. I will then write the script and save it as video12.docx, and also find a suitable background music for the video.I have learned some tips for creating a YouTube shorts script. Now, I am going to search for an interesting and unique topic in mythology that is not discussed much. After that, I will write a YouTube shorts script, identify an appropriate background music, and save the script and background music suggestion in separate files.saved google search results at googleresults0.txt to retrieve if shutdown randomly.I found a website with a list of common mythic themes and elements for certain world mythologies. I will browse this website to find an interesting and unique topic for a YouTube shorts script. Afterward, I will write the script, save it as video12.docx, identify an appropriate genre and background music, and write the music suggestion in a separate document.saved the scraping of the website results at websiteresults1.txt to retrieve if shutdown randomly.I have found an interesting and unique topic in Japanese mythology: the mysterious Tengu. I have written a YouTube shorts script about the Tengu and saved it as video12.docx. The next steps are to identify an appropriate genre and background music, read and output video12.docx, and write the background music suggestion in suggestion.docx.I have written 167 words for the YouTube shorts script on the mysterious Tengu of Japan, saved as video12.docx. Now, I will read video12.docx, identify an appropriate genre and background music for the video, and write the background music suggestion in a separate document.I have read the YouTube shorts script about the mysterious Tengu of Japan. Now, I will search for an appropriate background music genre for the mythology video, research a suitable song within that genre, and write the background music suggestion in suggestion.docx.saved google search results at googleresults2.txt to retrieve if shutdown randomly.I am searching for another unique and interesting topic in mythology to create another YouTube shorts script. I will be writing a script that will give a 40 seconds long video with a title, and identifying a suitable background music genre and song suggestion.saved google search results at googleresults3.txt to retrieve if shutdown randomly.I am browsing a website with 50 essential facts about mythology to find a unique and interesting topic for the YouTube shorts script. The next steps involve writing the script, saving it as video12.docx, and identifying the best background music genre and song suggestion.saved the scraping of the website results at websiteresults4.txt to retrieve if shutdown randomly.I have saved 10 interesting mythological facts from various cultures in my memory with the key ‘50_mythology_facts’. Now, I will choose a unique and interesting topic and write a YouTube shorts script about it. Once the script is written, I will save it as video12.docx, read it, and then determine the best background music genre and song suggestion.I have chosen the Bushmen myth as the topic for the second YouTube shorts script. I am writing the script and saving it as video12.docx. After that, I will read the script, determine the best background music genre, research a suitable song, and write the song suggestion in suggestion.docx.I have written 95 words for the YouTube shorts script about the Bushmen myth and saved it as video12.docx. I am now reading the script to understand the theme and tone, which will help me determine the best background music genre and select a suitable song suggestion for the video.I have read the YouTube shorts script about the Bushmen myth. Now, I am searching for the best genre for background music for mythological stories to complement the content and enhance the viewers’ experience. After identifying the genre, I will research a suitable song and write the song suggestion in suggestion.docx.saved google search results at googleresults5.txt to retrieve if shutdown randomly.I am browsing a website discussing the 5 best genres for background music for videos to determine a suitable genre for the Bushmen myth YouTube shorts script. After choosing the right genre, I will research an appropriate song and write the song suggestion in suggestion.docx.saved the scraping of the website results at websiteresults6.txt to retrieve if shutdown randomly.
The Task:make another youtube shorts script and retrieve how to make a script from memory with key shorts_script_tips and search something interesting in mythology that is not dicussed much after doing many indepth researches to determine a topic which is an interesting fact, reassure by making more and more research that this is a unique topic and write a script about it that will give a 40 seconds long video with a title and save it in video12.docx, and read the file video12.docx which is a script for a youtube short, then determine the genre that will be best for the video as a background music, and research what would be a great song to play in the background with no lyrics to be suitable in the video as a background music so that will let the retention of the user with the video, and then write the suggestion of the song with its link in suggestion.docx

1. Titans were the 12 gods who ruled the world before the Olympians
2. Heaven for Vikings was Valhalla
3. Olympians were the most important Greek gods, with Zeus as their king
4. In Abrahamic myth, all humans are descended from Adam and Eve
5. Rome is named after its mythical founder Romulus
6. In Powhatan myth, the world was created by a hare
7. The most famous British mythical hero is King Arthur
8. Chang’e is the Chinese moon goddess
9. In Bushmen myth, men and animals once lived in harmony underground
10. Ethiopia’s royal family claims descent from the mythical Queen of Sheba
System: Added to memory proceed with your plan.

1. Titans ruled the world before Olympians
2. Valhalla: Heaven for Vikings
3. Olympians: Most important Greek gods
4. Abrahamic myth: Human descent from Adam and Eve
5. Rome’s mythical founder: Romulus
6. Powhatan myth: World creation by a hare
7. British mythical hero: King Arthur
8. Chinese moon goddess: Chang’e
9. Bushmen myth: Men and animals in harmony underground
10. Ethiopia’s royal family: Descendants of Queen of Sheba
